title: Fix timesheet add-project search: project_register_items.completed → status
description: TimesheetGrid queries project_register_items.completed but column was replaced with status enum (active|complete|cancelled). Result: data returns null and project picker is empty. Fix: select status, derive completed = (status !== 'active').
close_reason: Swapped TimesheetGrid select from completed bool to status enum; derive completed = (status !== 'active'). Build passes.

title: Add /auth/callback route to portal app to work around Supabase allowlist matcher
description: Portal home page passes redirectTo='/' to AuthButton, which makes the Supabase redirect_to=http://<host>:3000/ (bare root). Supabase's allowlist matcher silently fails to match http://<ip>:<port>/** against bare-root URLs and falls back to Site URL — causing test VM (.95) traffic to bounce to production (.75). Fix: introduce a /auth/callback route in the portal app, repoint AuthButton to use it as redirect_to, and add the matching allowlist entries in Supabase. Other apps already use path-based redirectTo so they're unaffected.

title: Deploy pss-assembly-viewer canary to Pi and smoke test
description: Deploy the extracted assembly-viewer alongside the live monorepo version using docker-compose.canary.yml. Canary on host port 3107, off platform_net. Live route /assembly/ via gateway must be untouched. Smoke test: page loads at http://10.0.0.75:3107/assembly/, login works, 3D STL viewer renders, API routes (/api/assembly-data, /api/drawing, /api/export-pdf, /api/stl/*) respond, sidebar renders.
acceptance_criteria: Canary container running, healthcheck green, /assembly/ routes render at :3107, parity with live verified, no impact to live :3000/assembly/.

title: Land standalone-mode + extraction docs in platform-portal
description: Commit and push the in-monorepo standalone target, build script branching, and the extraction-guide / skill-doc updates produced during the assembly-viewer extraction. Files: docs/APP_EXTRACTION_GUIDE.md, docs/SKILL_pss_standalone_app.md, docker/node/Dockerfile.prod, scripts/deploy.sh, scripts/deploy-timed.sh, apps/assembly-viewer/next.config.ts, scripts/build-times.csv.
close_reason: Closed

title: Deploy Claude Design step 1: photos, AmbientBackdrop, sign-in, home hero, sidebar
description: Act on design guide/DEPLOY.md. Six steps: resize+copy 3 photos to apps/portal/public/photos/, add packages/ui/AmbientBackdrop.tsx + export, restyle sign-in in apps/portal/app/page.tsx, add hero strip to portal home, polish .pss-sidebar gradient in packages/ui/platform.css. Parent: platform-portal-4kp.
notes: Committed as 1781d5d. Steps 1-5 of design guide/DEPLOY.md applied. Step 6 (UTF-8) is a future-HTML reminder, no action needed. Step 7 (topbar, per-app accents) explicitly deferred. Photos resized via Pillow (no ImageMagick on this machine): home-01 7.3MB->459KB, aerial-01 8.0MB->473KB, signin-01 copied as-is at 100KB. Caveat: signin-01 source is only 400x600 and gets upscaled to fill the viewport — reads OK under the heavy darken+overlay but should be swapped for a larger crop when available. Portal build green in 5.5s.
close_reason: Closed

title: Create portal design brief for Claude Design
description: Produce a design brief MD file (modern tech feel, SpaceX/NASA inspired) to feed into Claude Design. Output: design-guide/ folder with brief.md covering brand voice, palette, typography, components, accessibility, motion, references, and current portal context. Outcome will be a generated CSS/Tailwind framework for portal apps.
notes: Brief written to design guide/design-brief.md. Decisions captured: dual-theme (light default), keep PSS navy + sky as locked brand tokens but refresh accents toward technical cyan/electric-blue, full design system + Storybook scope. Brief covers: brand non-negotiables, SpaceX/NASA aesthetic direction with take-from/leave guidance, full token spec (colour/type/spacing/radius/shadow/motion/icon), component inventory (existing to restyle + 19 new patterns), key screens, accessibility (WCAG AA both themes, kiosk readability at 3m), tech constraints (Next 16/React 19/Tailwind v4 CSS-first/pnpm workspaces), deliverables, repo file map, out-of-scope, acceptance criteria. Ready to hand to Claude Design with the GitHub repo URL.

title: Settings page: Materials CRUD for laser_material rows
description:
Repo: platform-portal
File: apps/laserquote/app/settings/page.tsx

Today: Settings only surfaces a hardcoded list of laser_rate keys. laser_material rows can only be added/edited via direct SQL. Operators have no UI to add a new grade (Galvanised, S690, brass, etc.) or update a rate.

Build a 'Materials' section on the Settings page:
- List active rows from laser_material (class, grade, rate, density, active toggle)
- Edit rate, density inline (same UX as existing rate editor)
- Add new row form: pick class from existing classes (dropdown), grade text input, rate, density (optional)
- Soft-delete via active=false toggle (don't hard delete — historic quotes reference grades)
- Normalise on save: grade.trim().toUpperCase() — prevents the case/whitespace bug class entirely

Also fix display lookup at apps/laserquote/app/imports/[id]/page.tsx:184-194:
- defaultMaterialRate currently uses hardcoded if/else mapping (mat,grade) -> rate_* key.
- Replace with the same pattern as pricing.ts: load laser_material, find by grade match (case-insens), fall back to rate_* keys.

Optional follow-up (separate bead?): retire rate_mild/rate_304/rate_316/rate_al from laser_rate seed once Materials CRUD is in place and all classes have laser_material coverage. Keep burden_rate, density_*, margins, thresholds.

Parent: platform-portal-ffn

title: pricing.ts: case-insensitive + whitespace-tolerant grade lookup
description:
Repo: pss-document-service (NOT platform-portal)
File: src/laser/pricing.ts:124-128

Bug: findMaterialGrade() uppercases the input grade then does strict equality against laser_material.grade. If a row stores 'Galvanised' (mixed case) or '5083\n' (whitespace), it never matches. Fall-through path silently returns the class default rate from laser_rate (e.g. rate_mild £950) — pricing wrong but no error.

Root cause for current incident: user inserted row with grade='Galvanised'. Engine looked for 'GALVANISED', no match, fell through to rate_mild. £1000/tonne intent, £950/tonne reality.

Mitigation already shipped: DB cleanup normalised existing rows (Galvanised -> GALVANISED, '5083\n' -> '5083').

Proper fix:
1. In findMaterialGrade, compare with .trim().toUpperCase() on both sides:
     materials.find(m => m.grade.trim().toUpperCase() === g)
2. Same for the .includes() loop — normalise m.grade.
3. Consider a DB trigger or app-layer normalisation on insert/update to prevent dirty data recurring (probably better as part of platform-portal-XXX Settings CRUD bead).

Test: add fixture rows with mixed-case/whitespace grades, verify quote pricing picks the row's rate not the class fallback.

Parent: platform-portal-ffn
notes: Moved to pss-document-service beads workspace as pss-document-service-9r2 (work lives in that repo). Closing this duplicate.
close_reason: Closed

title: LaserQuote: material data is split across two unconnected tables
description:
Adding a new material grade requires changes in 3 places that aren't connected:

1. laser_material table (migration 022) — drives ImportForm dropdowns, has its own 'rate' column. NEW grades go here.
2. laser_rate table (migration 021) — key/value rates with hardcoded keys (rate_mild, rate_304, rate_316, rate_al). Drives the Settings page AND the actual pricing lookup.
3. Hardcoded if/else in apps/laserquote/app/imports/[id]/page.tsx:186-193 mapping (material_class, grade) to a laser_rate key.

Symptom: User added laser_material row for MILD/GALVANISED. Row exists, but:
- Doesn't appear in ImportForm pick list (cause TBD — cache or active flag, separate from the architecture issue)
- Doesn't appear in Rates & Settings (by design — Settings only reads hardcoded laser_rate keys)
- Wouldn't price correctly even if selected (pricing if/else has no GALVANISED branch)

The laser_material.rate column appears to be dead data — pricing path never reads it.

Two fix options:
A) MINIMAL: insert rate_galvanised into laser_rate, add GALVANISED branch to pricing if/else, add key to Settings group config. Ships galvanised quickly but perpetuates the dual-table mess.
B) PROPER: finish the migration to data-driven pricing — pricing reads laser_material.rate, Settings page reads laser_material rows, retire laser_rate's rate_* keys (keep burden_rate, density_*, margins, thresholds since those aren't per-grade). Then any new material is one row insert.
notes:
Investigation findings:

**Pricing engine already partly data-driven.** It lives in pss-document-service/src/laser/pricing.ts (NOT in platform-portal). loadMaterials() reads laser_material; findMaterialGrade() tries exact grade match first, falls back to class default; getDensity() and getMaterialRate() check laser_material.rate/density before falling back to laser_rate.rate_*.

This means a laser_material row for MILD/GALVANISED with a rate WILL price correctly via the engine — IF it's reached. The hardcoded if/else I flagged in apps/laserquote/app/imports/[id]/page.tsx:186-193 only affects the *display* of 'default rate' on the import detail page, not actual quote pricing.

**Real gaps:**
1. Settings page (apps/laserquote/app/settings/page.tsx) has hardcoded keys list — never surfaces laser_material rows for editing. To add/edit galvanised via UI, need a Materials CRUD section on Settings.
2. imports/[id]/page.tsx defaultMaterialRate display uses stale hardcoded if/else — should use same laser_material lookup as the engine.
3. laser_rate.rate_mild/304/316/al are duplicated by laser_material rows seeded in migration 022 — they're now fallback-only. Could be retired, or kept as last-resort defaults.
4. ImportForm pick list issue is likely separate (cache or active=false) — needs user diagnostic before assuming code bug.

**Cross-repo fix touches:**
- platform-portal: Settings page CRUD for laser_material; fix imports display lookup; possibly migration to drop rate_* from laser_rate seed.
- pss-document-service: optionally remove the hardcoded fallbacks in pricing.ts so laser_material is the sole source of truth.

title: Timesheets: deactivated employees vanish from history/wages/status panel
description:
When an employee is marked active=false, several display contexts filter them out using .eq('active', true), causing historic data to disappear. Active flag should only gate future entry (the EmployeePicker dropdown), never historic display.

Affected files:
- apps/timesheets/components/EmployeeStatusPanel.tsx:40 — side panel hides deactivated employees even for past weeks where they logged hours
- apps/timesheets/app/reports/page.tsx:155-158, 918 — activeEmployeeCount used as denominator for historic weekly approval ratio (retroactively changes past weeks; can falsely show 'all approved')
- apps/timesheets/app/reports/page.tsx:235-238 — Bradford drops deactivated employees entirely (debatable; needs explicit decision)
- apps/timesheets/app/wages/page.tsx:89 — historic wage prep loses deactivated employees, breaking payroll reconstruction for past weeks

Project hour totals on the report grid are NOT actually missing hours (raw entries query has no active filter at reports/page.tsx:122-130) — but the side panel and wages page drop rows, which feels like missing hours.

Fix approach (Option A, minimal, no schema change):
- Status panel: load all employees, group as 'active no hours / needs approval / approved / inactive (had hours this week)'. Only show inactive when they have entries or approval that week.
- Reports approval denominator: count employees who had any hours that week, not current actives.
- Wages page: include any employee with timesheet entries that week regardless of active.
- Bradford: explicit choice — keep current actives only (label 'Current employees') or include all in window.

EmployeePicker.tsx:24 should keep its active=true filter (correct: gates new entry).
notes: Code changes pushed in commit 0c98c5a. Pending verification on Pi after deploy. Bradford metric still scopes to current actives by design — flag if that needs to change too.
close_reason: Closed

title: Scanner UI files renamed for asset → subject
description: The scanner UI files in apps/scanner/ were renamed in commit 2912edb to use 'subject_register' / 'subject_code' instead of 'asset_register' / 'asset_code', as part of a terminology cleanup coordinated with pss-document-service-weo. Files affected: app/forms/page.tsx, app/history/page.tsx, components/AssetTable.tsx (still named that — internal cosmetic rename deferred), components/RecentJobs.tsx, components/RefileDialog.tsx. Pairs with Supabase migration 033_rename_asset_to_subject.sql in supabase/migrations/. Both code and migration must be deployed together — applying migration without redeploying the scanner UI breaks asset_register lookups; redeploying the UI without the migration breaks subject_register lookups. Coordinated cutover happening 2026-05-04 (holiday) — see the doc-service-side issue tracker for full context.

title: Split env into dev (self-hosted Supabase) and prod (cloud); scrub local secrets; de-hardcode pi-deploy.sh
description:
# Why

Wire platform-portal's dev path to the new self-hosted Supabase (http://10.0.0.85:8000)
without risking dev values leaking into production image builds. Also clean up two
working-tree-only secret leaks (service-role key in .env / .env.production.example /
portainer.env) and a soft anon-key hardcode in scripts/pi-deploy.sh.

History review showed the service-role key was NEVER committed (gitignore did its
job — .env.* covers .env.production.example). Anon key is in pi-deploy.sh on main
but is public-safe by Supabase design. So no history rewrite, no force-push.

# What

Env file layout (all in repo root):
- Rename .env -> .env.production (existing real cloud values; gitignored)
- New .env.development (self-hosted Supabase values; gitignored; populated from
  pss-supabase-host/connections.md)
- New env.production.example (committed; placeholders only — note the no-leading-dot
  matches the existing env.example convention since .gitignore has .env.*)
- New env.development.example (committed; placeholders only)
- Keep a copy of .env.production as .env for backward-compat with standalone-app
  pattern (pss-matl-cert and future repos reference ../platform-portal/.env). File
  bd follow-up to migrate standalone repos off this alias.

Selection rules (every consumer is explicit; default is "fail loudly"):
- docker-compose.yml (dev): env_file: .env.development on all 9 services
- scripts/deploy.sh and scripts/deploy-timed.sh: source .env.production; hard-fail
  if missing (no silent fallback to empty NEXT_PUBLIC_* vars)
- scripts/pi-deploy.sh: replace inline hardcoded cloud values with
  source .env.production
- docker-compose.prod.yml / .deploy.yml: no structural change (read shell env at
  compose-eval time); document "source .env.production first" in README
- docker-compose.vm.yml: no change (Portainer injects env at stack level)

Override path (rare — dev against cloud):
- New docker-compose.cloud.yml that flips env_file: .env.production for each service.
  Run with: docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.cloud.yml up

Secret scrubbing (working tree only — these files were never committed):
- .env.production: keep real cloud values (it's the canonical for prod builds)
- .env.production.example -> rewrite as committed env.production.example with
  placeholders. Delete the gitignored .env.production.example.
- portainer.env: leave intact (gitignored, used for cloud Portainer stack imports).
  Add a note in docs that this is "cloud" — out of scope for the dev/prod split.

Docs:
- README section: "Dev vs prod env" — which file each path uses, how to flip,
  off-LAN VPN+jump-host caveat for reaching 10.0.0.85 from outside the LAN.
- README section: "Self-hosted dev key rotation" — when pss-supabase-host rotates
  via gen_secrets.py: regenerate connections.md, update .env.development, restart
  dev compose. Cloud rotation is a separate process.
- Update docs/skill_pss_standalone_app.md invariant 4 to reference .env.production
  as the canonical (and note the .env compat alias).

Out of scope (deferred to follow-up issues):
- Cycling the service-role key (deferred per stakeholder; it never hit history)
- Migrating standalone repos off the .env compat alias
- Cleaning the anon key out of historical commits (public-safe; not worth force-push)
acceptance_criteria:
- .env.development exists locally with self-hosted values from connections.md
- .env.production exists locally with the existing cloud values (renamed from .env)
- .env exists as a copy of .env.production (compat alias)
- env.development.example and env.production.example committed with placeholder values
- docker-compose.yml dev services all reference .env.development
- scripts/deploy.sh, deploy-timed.sh, pi-deploy.sh source .env.production and fail
  loudly if missing; pi-deploy.sh no longer hardcodes any values
- docker-compose.cloud.yml exists for the rare dev-against-cloud case
- README documents dev vs prod selection, the override, key rotation, and VPN caveat
- skill_pss_standalone_app.md invariant 4 updated
- bd follow-up issues filed for: standalone-app migration, key rotation

title: Move all apps to a uniform /auth/callback?next=<path> pattern
description: Follow-up to platform-portal-pw1. Refactor AuthButton so every app uses a stable /auth/callback route as the Supabase redirect_to, with the post-login destination encoded in a ?next= query param. Eliminates the class of bugs where a caller passes a wildcard-unfriendly redirectTo. Touches every app's route tree, so plan it as a coordinated change rather than urgent fix. Other apps work today via their path-based redirectTo, so this is a P2 cleanup, not a fire.

title: Persist incoterms and lead_time on laser quote import
description: Lead time and incoterms were rendering blank on auto-generated laser quote PDFs. The /api/upload route at apps/laserquote/app/api/upload/route.ts collected both fields from the form and forwarded them to the laserquote service, but did not write them to the laser_import row. The PDF backend reads from imp.lead_time / imp.incoterms, so without the columns being populated the values came through empty. Schema columns (incoterms, lead_time — text, nullable) were already in place. Fix: add incoterms and lead_time to the laser_import insert payload (default incoterms to EXW, lead_time to null when missing).
close_reason: Closed

title: Portainer-on-VM deployment from Git repo (no ghcr, separate from Pi path)
description: Stand up an alternate deployment path for platform-portal where Portainer on a Proxmox VM clones the GitHub repo and builds images locally via a compose file's build: directives. Replaces the dev-machine -> ghcr.io -> Pi flow with a single source -> VM-builds -> runs flow. Reuses existing docker/node/Dockerfile.prod (runner / runner-standalone targets). Coexists with the Pi/ghcr path; does not replace it.
design: Add docker-compose.portainer.yml mirroring docker-compose.deploy.yml services but swapping image: refs for build: blocks targeting Dockerfile.prod with APP_NAME/APP_PORT/NEXT_PUBLIC_* build args. NEXT_PUBLIC_* vars supplied via Portainer stack env (read at build time, baked into images). Gateway uses docker/nginx/Dockerfile.prod. Standalone-mode apps (currently assembly-viewer) use target: runner-standalone; rest use runner. Rewrite docs/portainer-deployment.md for Git-pull-and-build flow (no registry section).
acceptance_criteria: 1) docker-compose.portainer.yml builds and runs all services on an amd64 VM via Portainer Stack -> Repository mode. 2) NEXT_PUBLIC_* vars correctly baked in from Portainer-supplied env. 3) docs/portainer-deployment.md rewritten with Git-pull-and-build instructions, env var table, and redeploy workflow. 4) Existing ghcr-based docker-compose.deploy.yml + scripts/deploy.sh remain untouched and functional for the Pi path.
notes: Open questions before implementation: (a) Proxmox VM architecture (assumed amd64 - confirm not ARM); (b) GitHub repo public vs private (affects whether Portainer needs a PAT in Git creds). First build on VM will be slow (5-10+ min full pnpm install + 9 next builds); subsequent builds should hit Docker layer cache for unchanged apps. User memory 'don't build on Pi, use ghcr.io' is Pi-specific (slow ARM); a reasonable amd64 VM is fine for local builds.
close_reason: VM deploy successful on docker01 (80 GB disk). docker-compose.vm.yml builds and runs all services via Portainer Stack repository mode. Docs and prerequisites updated to reflect 50+ GB disk requirement learned during this rollout.

title: Deviations page at /timesheets/reports/variance
description: New filterable, sortable table page surfacing weekly variance snapshots from timesheet_approvals. Read-only view; data source is straight SELECT from timesheet_approvals joined to employees - no live RPC calls at view time. Existing approvals with NULL variance columns are filtered out by default.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Route: /timesheets/reports/variance. (2) Columns: employee name, week starting, clocked h, allocated work h, variance (signed, colour-banded), incomplete-clocking flag, approved by, approved at. (3) Filters: date range (default last 8 weeks), employee picker, min abs(variance) (default 30 min), include/exclude incomplete-clocking (default exclude). (4) Default sort: most recent week first; secondary by abs(variance) desc within week. (5) Click any row navigates to /timesheets/?employee=ID&week=YYYY-MM-DD. (6) Rows where variance_minutes IS NULL excluded by default (legacy approvals). (7) No CSV export. (8) No role gate beyond authenticated (matches existing pages).
close_reason: Closed

title: EmployeeStatusPanel: weekly variance badge
description: Variance badge on each employee row in EmployeeStatusPanel, alongside the existing Bradford pill. Triage view so the foreman can spot meaningful variances before opening any grid. Independent fetch from grid (no shared state).
acceptance_criteria: (1) One additional fetch in the panel effect: employees_daily_hours() for the visible week, all employees. (2) Badge shown only when employee has at least one tap in the week AND abs(weekly variance) > 30 min. (3) Colour bands at the weekly level: 30min-1h amber, >1h red. (4) Format like 'D-1.25h' / 'D+2.5h' (signed decimal hours with delta prefix). (5) Tooltip shows clocked / allocated / variance with minute precision. (6) Position: same row as identity / Bradford / hours pills.
close_reason: Implemented in 18d483a, evaluated in practice, reverted in 715872b. Foreman feedback: badge didn't surface anything not already visible in the grid, and added panel clutter without proportional triage value. Decision: don't pursue.

title: TimesheetGrid: Clocked & Variance rows + approval-bar headline + snapshot on approve
description: Per-day Clocked and Variance summary rows in TimesheetGrid below the existing Overtime row. Approval bar gets a compact variance headline. On Approve, variance components snapshot to the approval row. Variance fires only when the employee has any tap in the week (otherwise UI is unchanged from today). Clocked source: employees_daily_hours() RPC from the presence schema (read-only contract, no modifications). Allocated total excludes SICK-01, HOLIDAY-01, TRAINING-01.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Clocked + Variance rows render only when employee has any tap that week; otherwise both hidden. (2) Clocked row shows per-day decimal hours from RPC. (3) Variance row shows signed decimal per day; colour bands: abs delta <=15min neutral, 15-30min amber, >30min red. (4) Tooltip on each Variance cell shows clocked/allocated/variance with minute precision. (5) Variance Total column shows the weekly figure. (6) Approval bar shows compact 'Variance: -1h25 over 38h clocked' line next to existing approval state. (7) On Approve, write clocked_minutes, allocated_work_minutes, variance_minutes (signed = clocked-allocated), incomplete_clocking to the approval row. (8) Re-approval refreshes snapshot from current data.
close_reason: Closed

title: Migration: add variance columns to timesheet_approvals
description: Add 4 nullable columns to timesheet_approvals to support the timecard variance cross-check feature. Foundational for the rest of the feature work. The timesheet system snapshots variance between allocated project hours and presence-clocked hours at approval time, for traceability and trend analysis via a deviations report.
acceptance_criteria: New migration in supabase/migrations/. ALTER TABLE adds: clocked_minutes integer, allocated_work_minutes integer, variance_minutes integer (clocked minus allocated, signed), incomplete_clocking boolean default false. All nullable so existing rows are unaffected. No backfill of historical approval rows.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add /employee-presence to dev gateway (default.conf)
description: Local dev gateway uses docker/nginx/default.conf, not production.conf — was missing the employee-presence redirect + proxy so localhost:3000/employee-presence fell through to portal upstream and Next rendered a styled 404. Mirror the matl-cert dev block (proxy to host IP 10.0.0.75:3011).
close_reason: Closed

title: Add Employee Presence tile to portal home
description: Portal home (apps/portal/app/page.tsx) has a hardcoded apps[] array; employee-presence was missing so the gateway route works but there's no link. Add the tile and rebuild+redeploy the portal image.
close_reason: Closed

title: Laser dashboard: device_status RLS blocks frontend reads, header always shows Offline
description: device_status has a single RLS policy 'service_role_only' for ALL commands. The Mazak laser dashboard reads device_status from the browser (authenticated role), so the read returns empty, device is null, and the header always renders 'Offline' regardless of the actual status. Add a SELECT policy for the authenticated role so logged-in users can see device statuses, matching the pattern used on the laser_* tables.
close_reason: Migration 028 added; policy applied to live db; dashboard should now reflect online status

title: Laser dashboard: turnover chart should mirror table's value sharing
description: The runs table now shares quote_total across visible end-of-cycle runs and shows drafts. The Daily Turnover chart still uses the stricter 'confirmed-only + first run gets full value' rule, so the chart shows nothing where the table has values. Align them: chart should include any alive (non-lost/non-cancelled) quote and split totals across the end-of-cycle runs in the 30-day window, summing per-day shares.
close_reason: Chart now uses same alive-quote matching and per-run share split as the table

title: Laser dashboard: end-of-cycle quote display, share value across runs, no scroll-reset on poll
description: (1) Quote/Value should only render on end-of-cycle rows (ended_state=READY AND runtime>=30s); FEED_HOLD/INTERRUPTED/STOPPED and false starts get '—'. (2) For a quote with N visible end-of-cycle runs in the date range, each row shows totalValue/N rather than the full total — so the column sums to the quote total. (3) Polling every 20s currently flips runsLoading=true which removes the table, shrinks the page, and jerks scroll to top. Suppress the loading flag on poll refreshes — only show the spinner on first load or when the date range changes.
close_reason: End-of-cycle gate, value-sharing memo, silent polls

title: Laser dashboard: include draft quotes with status badge, keep turnover confirmed-only
description: The strict 'confirmed orders only' rule was too tight — 114/121 quotes are draft, so no Quote/Value showed up. Loosen the runs-table match to include any non-lost/non-cancelled quote and surface a small status badge so operators can see draft vs won/delivered at a glance. The Daily Turnover chart should keep its strict rule (only confirmed orders count toward revenue), so ambition-stage drafts don't inflate the numbers.
close_reason: Drafts shown with badge; turnover stays confirmed-only

title: Wire employee-presence into gateway + sync standalone-app doc
description: Add /employee-presence proxy block + redirect to docker/nginx/production.conf, rebuild+push gateway image, and pull three doc updates (Portainer-friendly compose, Portainer Stack from Git deploy section, Next.js production-build gotchas) from upstream pss-employee-presence/NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md back into platform-portal/docs/NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md. Output Pi-side redeploy commands at the end.
close_reason: Closed

title: Laser dashboard: strip file extension when matching program names
description: Mazak reports program names like 'P11819 11819.NC' (with extension). laser_program.program_name from CSV imports has no extension ('P11819 11819'). Current ilike match fails as a result. Strip trailing .XX from both sides in normaliseProgram so matches work regardless of which side has the extension.
close_reason: Extension regex stripped in normaliseProgram; both sides keyed identically

title: Laser dashboard: restrict quote linkage to confirmed orders only
description: Both the runs table Quote/Value columns and the daily turnover chart should only attribute against confirmed orders (status in won, completed, ready_for_collection, delivered). Drafts, issued, revised, error, lost, cancelled should not show a quote linkage so they don't count as turnover.
close_reason: Confirmed-only rule applied via QUOTE_CONFIRMED_STATUSES set; both table and chart use the same fetchQuoteMatches

title: Laser dashboard: link program runs to quotes and add daily turnover chart
description: Cross-reference pss_laser_program_runs.program against laser_program.program_name to surface the originating laser_quote for each run. Add Quote (number, link to import detail) and Value columns to the runs table. Add a 30-day Daily Turnover bar chart alongside Cutting Hours. Casing differs (.NC vs .nc) so match case-insensitively. Quotes get attributed once each on the first day a matching program run completed (>=30s, ended_state in READY/FEED_HOLD); subsequent days don't re-attribute that quote.
close_reason: Quote linkage in runs table + daily turnover chart shipped

title: Laser header: dim stale state values when device is offline
description: Header shows execution/program/mode/material as if live even when device_status.status='offline'. Should dim/mark these as last-known when offline so operators don't trust stale state.
close_reason: Header values dimmed and labelled as last-known when device offline

title: Rebuild Laser dashboard against pss_laser_* schema
description: The Mazak/Laser dashboard at /laserquote/mazak was built against the old mazak_* tables. The confirmed schema uses pss_laser_program_runs, pss_laser_state_events, pss_laser_telemetry with revised columns and semantics. Rebuild as a parameterised route /laserquote/machines/[deviceId] supporting MAZAK-SN328525 today and additional lasers later. Add comment parsing (MATERIAL/dims regex), gas badge from material suffix, NCmode in header, false-start filter (default on), revised utilisation logic (cutting time sums READY+FEED_HOLD ended_states, programs counts distinct programs with >=30s rows, parts = max-min part_count_end over range).
close_reason: Page moved to /laserquote/machines/[deviceId], rebuilt against pss_laser_* schema with comment parsing, gas badge, NCmode, false-start filter, revised utilisation maths, 30-day chart on READY+FEED_HOLD only

title: Add scripts/new-app.sh bootstrap helper
description: CLI script: prompts for a folder name (pss-<appname>), creates C:\Dev\PSS\<name>, copies docs/NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md, writes .claude/CLAUDE.md so Claude auto-reads it on session start, runs bd init in the new folder, opens the folder in a new VS Code window with the doc pre-opened, then opens a Claude Code chat with a pre-filled prompt to scaffold the app.
close_reason: Closed

title: Author NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md starter doc
description: Self-contained scaffolding doc the user can copy into a new pss-<appname> repo. Covers port reservation, file templates (package.json, next.config.ts, Dockerfile, .dockerignore, docker-compose.app.yml, build.sh), gateway wiring, Pi deploy, and an AI-assistant-instructions section. Includes reminders to bd init in the new repo and to point the assistant at beads tooling.
close_reason: Closed

title: Remove apps/assembly-viewer from platform-portal monorepo
description: After canary validation passes: delete apps/assembly-viewer/ from the monorepo, remove the service block from docker-compose.yml / deploy.yml / prod.yml, remove from the loop in scripts/deploy.sh, update docs/PORTS.md row to point at the standalone repo. Promote the canary to be the live stack (rename container, join platform_net, drop the host port mapping so it goes through gateway). Verify gateway /assembly/ still routes correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Monorepo no longer contains apps/assembly-viewer/. Live /assembly/ via gateway resolves to the standalone container on platform_net. No regressions in other apps.

title: 24h soak: monitor canary stability and parity vs live
description: Watch the canary for 24h after deploy. Confirm: no container restarts, healthcheck stays green, no errors in docker logs, no user complaints. Compare behaviour against live. If anything diverges, file a bug and gate the monorepo removal.

title: Laser timeline: distinguish STOPPED from INTERRUPTED with black fill
description: STOPPED and INTERRUPTED both render in red on the execution timeline so they can't be told apart at a glance. Change STOPPED to black/dark-grey so INTERRUPTED's red stays the unmistakable 'something went wrong' colour.
close_reason: STOPPED -> #1f2937 fill / gray-800 badge; INTERRUPTED stays red

title: Quote/Import detail: show effective Sheet Price / Material Rate, mark N/A on free-issue
description: Currently Sheet Price and Material Rate cells show '—' when no override was provided, leaving operators unsure what rate was actually used. Improvements: when free_issue, label both 'Not Applicable' (no material charged). When not free_issue and no override given, look up the default material rate from laser_rate (keyed by material/grade) and display it so the operator can see what the calculation actually used. When an override is set, keep showing the override.
close_reason: Sheet Price / Material Rate cells now show effective values with override/default/N-A semantics

title: Quote/Import detail: show free_issue and rem_charge flags alongside premium
description: Import detail panel currently shows only the Premium flag. Sheet Price and Material Rate cells are often blank because free_issue imports don't carry a material price — without seeing the free_issue flag the operator can't tell why. Add Free Issue and Remnant Charge alongside Premium, ideally as small status pills in a single Flags cell so the panel doesn't grow another row.
close_reason: Flags pill cell on import detail panel

title: Re-nest support: track replacing/replaced programs with self-FK on laser_program
description:
Operator workflow: when material is re-nested onto a smaller plate (e.g. offcut), the new program ends up with a different program_name and the laser-dashboard quote linkage breaks. Plan: append the re-nest to the existing quote/import (no recalc, no revision) and add self-referential FKs on laser_program — replaces_program_id and/or replaced_by_program_id — so we can trace the chain. The existing match-program-to-import logic already handles this once the new program is added under the same import_id.

Pending discussion with operator before scoping. Open questions:
- Does the re-nest carry over the original part list verbatim or sometimes drop parts?
- Where in the UI does the operator declare 're-nest of program X'? Library page action, or a fresh import flow with a 're-nest of...' picker?
- Do we want the dashboard runs table to show 'P11849 (re-nest of P11819)' or just resolve silently to the parent quote?

title: Timeline: render 06:00–17:00 working-hours window
description: Execution timeline currently spans full 24h. Show working-day window (06:00–17:00 London) instead, with constants so the window can be changed later.
close_reason: Window 06-17, READY no fill

title: Roll out Next.js standalone output to remaining 8 monorepo apps
description: portal, jobcards, documents, timesheets, operations, scanner, laserquote, nesting all still use the legacy runner target. Each opt-in: add output: 'standalone' + outputFileTracingRoot to next.config.ts, add to STANDALONE_APPS list in scripts/deploy.sh and deploy-timed.sh. Expected savings ~45s per app on full rebuild and ~6 GB Pi disk usage. Pace one or two at a time so a regression is easy to attribute.

title: Decide on orphan/untracked files in platform-portal working tree
description: Items flagged but not actioned: (1) apps/assembly-viewer/C:DevPSSplatform-portal — looks like an accidentally-created directory with a literal Windows-path name; (2) apps/assembly-viewer/sample-data.json (4.5 MB) — unclear if test fixture or accidental dump; (3) tools/qrcodes/ — H&S/crane PNGs, generated artifacts vs canonical assets; (4) .vscode/ — IDE settings, depends on team policy. Each needs a yes/no on commit-or-gitignore.

value: Extraction rationale (2026-04-20 retro): the original thesis 'extraction cuts build time' was wrong — per-app build cost is dominated by next build and docker push regardless of repo location. Extraction is for LIFECYCLE ISOLATION (independent start/stop/rollback, separate release cadence) not speed. matl-cert was extracted for compliance reasons; pss-assembly-viewer for canary/cutover practice. Don't extract apps that share heavily with portal/jobcards.

Timesheet panel badge discipline (2026-04-29 sv5 retro): EmployeeStatusPanel badges should only surface signals that change which employee the foreman opens next (e.g. Bradford routes attention to absence patterns, hours pill flags zero/low). The variance badge was reverted because it duplicated information already visible in the grid when the employee is opened — adding triage clutter without proportional triage value. Rule of thumb: if the foreman would see the same signal one click later, don't put it in the panel.

Portainer-on-VM deploy gotchas (2026-04-30, learned during docker01 first deploy): (1) Disk: needs 50+ GB free (15 GB Ubuntu default fails). (2) platform_net must be created BEFORE deploy and may not survive reboots — add a systemd one-shot to recreate. (3) Re-pull image MUST be unticked in Portainer redeploy for VM path (images are local-only, never pushed) — only Re-build image. Re-pull causes 'pull access denied for platform-portal/<svc>'. (4) NEXT_PUBLIC_* are baked at build time; changing them needs a real rebuild AND removing the old images first ('docker images platform-portal/* -q | xargs docker rmi') because layer cache silently reuses old build layers. (5) HOST_DOC_ROOT must be a Linux path on the VM, never a Windows path copied from dev .env. (6) BuildKit corruption (unpigz crc32 mismatch) after disk-full events: 'docker builder prune -af'. (7) Supabase Auth → Redirect URLs allowlist must include http://<vm-ip>:<port>/** matching NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL exactly (scheme, host, port). (8) If Portainer says 'deployed successfully' but Stacks shows zero containers, the build succeeded but compose up failed silently — most likely platform_net missing.

Centralised .env convention (2026-04-20): platform-portal/.env is canonical for all projects. Siblings reference it via env_file: ../platform-portal/.env in their docker-compose.app.yml. On the Pi, layout is /opt/platform-portal/.env and /opt/pss-<app>/ as siblings. App-specific overrides (PORT, container-name) stay inline in the compose; cross-app secrets/URLs live in the shared file only. Edit secrets in one place.

Supabase allowlist matcher silently fails for ALL IPv4-literal+port redirect URLs — has been broken from the start, not a regression (2026-04-30, confirmed on docker01 deploy). Symptom: redirect_to=http://<ip>:<port>/ correctly sent in /authorize, but Supabase callback rewrites Location to whatever Site URL is configured as, regardless of whether the requested URL is in the allowlist (even with both /** wildcard AND bare entries). Site URL fallback has been doing 100% of the redirect work for IP+port URLs; allowlist entries with IPv4 literals are decorative. PROOF: flipping Site URL from http://10.0.0.75:3000 to http://10.0.0.95:3000 caused production (.75) traffic to bounce to .95 within seconds — symmetric breakage confirms allowlist match is genuinely failing for both IPs, only Site URL works. DO NOT use the Site URL flip as a workaround — it has full prod blast radius (we briefly broke prod doing exactly this and had to revert immediately). Real workarounds: (a) use a real hostname instead of an IP for non-prod environments — add DNS or /etc/hosts entry, set NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://<hostname>:<port>, add http://<hostname>:<port>/** to allowlist; hostnames empirically match where IPs don't. (b) use a separate Supabase project per environment — clean isolation, requires schema duplication. (c) file a Supabase support ticket with HAR/log artefacts; this is their bug. Diagnostic notes: GoTrue auth log 'referer' field is NOT the HTTP Referer header — it's an internal computed value (often Site URL) and reads identically across all requests, so don't rely on it for diagnosis. auth.flow_state table is empty for implicit-grant Azure flow (PKCE only), so server-side state isn't queryable that way. Bake-check command to verify NEXT_PUBLIC_* in a built portal:vm image: docker run --rm platform-portal/portal:vm sh -c 'grep -rohE "localtest|10\.0\.0\.[0-9]+" /repo/apps/portal/.next 2>/dev/null | sort -u'.

Portainer-on-VM deployment disk sizing (2026-04-30, confirmed working): a from-scratch build of platform-portal (9 apps) needs ~50+ GB free at deploy time. 15-20 GB VMs (Ubuntu Server defaults) fail mid-build with 'no space left on device' during layer extraction — Docker images alone hit 3.5 GB and build cache another 2 GB before any pnpm install completes. Steady state after success is ~10-15 GB. Recommended: 60-80 GB total VM disk. If using LVM and the VG has free space (check vgs), claim it with lvextend -l +100%FREE first before resizing the Proxmox virtual disk. After Proxmox resize: growpart /dev/sda 3, pvresize, lvextend, resize2fs. First successful deploy was on docker01 (80 GB).

Two distinct deployment paths for platform-portal (2026-04-29): (1) Pi path uses docker-compose.deploy.yml + scripts/deploy.sh, builds on dev machine, cross-compiles ARM64, distributes via ghcr.io/ukstevem/platform-portal/*, Pi pulls. Documented in docs/portainer-deployment.md. (2) VM path uses docker-compose.vm.yml, Portainer Stack repository mode, clones repo and builds locally on the VM (amd64), images tagged platform-portal/<svc>:vm and never pushed anywhere. Documented in docs/portainer-vm-deployment.md. Do not mix paths on the same host. Same NEXT_PUBLIC_* build args are required for both. The 'don't build on Pi' rule is Pi-specific (slow ARM); building on a reasonable amd64 VM is fine.

Standalone-mode rollout (2026-04-20): BuildKit cache patch eliminated the pnpm install tax (~42s/app saved on incremental). Next.js output: 'standalone' + outputFileTracingRoot dropped runtime image from ~800 MB to ~100 MB and push time from ~38s to ~7s. Total full-sweep time barely moved (~1200s) because next build + push still dominate per-app — that's the floor. The real win is on partial rebuilds (~5s for unchanged apps) and on Pi disk pressure.

Standalone repo trap (2026-04-20): app/.dockerignore is MANDATORY. Without it, host npm install creates Windows-path symlinks in node_modules for file: deps (@platform/ui/auth/supabase). 'COPY . .' in the builder stage overlays those broken symlinks on top of the container's clean node_modules and the build fails with 'Module not found: Can't resolve @platform/ui'. matl-cert avoided this by chance (no local node_modules existed when first built). Always include .dockerignore that excludes node_modules, .next, .env*, .git.
